comment_content: Volume 4 isn't bad, but it doesn't quite reach the heights of the previous volumes. It feels like a mixed bag — some episodes are interesting, but most don't have the same impact, creativity, or emotional depth as those from Volumes 1 to 3. The earlier seasons had more memorable stories and unique animation styles that stuck with me. Volume 4 is still worth watching for fans, but it just doesn’t feel as strong or innovative as the past ones.

comment_content:
😢😢👎👎 This season was an absolute disappointment. Netflix destroyed what once made Love, Death & Robots great. This volume lacks soul, risk, vision… everything.

I saw someone comment that “people just want blood and fights.” No — that’s not what this show was about. What made it special was the clever satire of “Three Robots,” the brutal beauty and mind-blowing ending of “The Witness,” or the raw, unmatched cyberpunk power of “Sonnie’s Edge,” which, in my opinion, remains unbeatable.

This season? Maybe “Mini Alien Invasion” was okay, and one or two others, but with pathetic runtimes of 7 minutes? Seriously? Is this all that’s left of a series that was supposed to push the boundaries of animation and storytelling?

A total mess. I honestly wish this season didn’t exist.
